steps.title: Check current cloud-native-network deployment
steps.description:
Verify your cloud-native-network version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cloud-native-network -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cloud-native-network
helm list -n cloud-native-network 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working cloud-native-network installation.

steps.title: Review cloud-native-network configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant cloud-native-network configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n cloud-native-network -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cloud-native-network
kubectl get configmap -n cloud-native-network -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=cloud-native-network
```
Hi, thanks for the project, it's very intresting!
I would like to work on seccomp feature if possible.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Add seccomp
steps.description:
Fix #25 

So this turns out to be simpler than I thought, thanks to the existance of `libseccomp`. `runc` also uses `libseccomp` which does all the heavy lifting.

One concern I have is the `seccomp-sys` repo. We don't have a lot of choices for a rust binding for libseccomp and this one looks reasonable. However, it is LGPL, so we may want to roll our own bindings. I used this repo just for prototype. If we are NOT OK with LGPL code into our repo, then we should discuss on what is the right

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cloud-native-network -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cloud-native-network
kubectl get events -n cloud-native-network --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Add seccomp" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Fix #25 

So this turns out to be simpler than I thought, thanks to the existance of `libseccomp`. `runc` also uses `libseccomp` which does all the heavy lifting.

One concern I have is the `seccomp-sys` repo. We don't have a lot of choices for a rust binding for libseccomp and this one looks reasonable. However, it is LGPL, so we may want to roll our own bindings.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with cloud-native-network installed or the issue environment reproducible.
